channel.title: NPR Topics: World
channel.description: NPR world news, international art and culture, world business and financial markets, world economy, and global trends in health, science and technology. Subscribe to the World Story of the Day podcast and RSS feed.
channel.copyright: Copyright 2024 NPR - For Personal Use Only

item.title: U.K. leaders call for calm as protests break out after Belfast street stabbing
item.description: U.K. leaders called for calm Tuesday after the arrest of a Sudanese man accused of trying to kill a man in a vicious stabbing on a Belfast street sparked fiery anti-immigration protests.
item.content:encoded:
U.K. leaders called for calm Tuesday after the arrest of a Sudanese man accused of trying to kill a man in a vicious stabbing on a Belfast street sparked fiery anti-immigration protests.

(Image credit: Peter Morrison)
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press

item.title: President Trump says U.S. 'must' respond to Iran attack that downed Army helicopter
item.description: Monday night, President Trump spoke optimistically about negotiations to end the war with Iran. Now, he has confirmed that Iran shot down a U.S. Army Apache helicopter, saying the U.S. must respond.
item.content:encoded: Monday night, President Trump spoke optimistically about negotiations to end the war with Iran. Now, he has confirmed that Iran shot down a U.S. Army Apache helicopter, saying the U.S. must respond.
item.dc:creator: Deepa Shivaram

item.title: 4 states head to the polls. And, global conflicts are on the rise, new report says
item.description: Voters in four states head to the polls today for their primaries. Here are the races to watch. And, global conflicts are at their highest level since World War II, data shows.
item.content:encoded:
Voters in four states head to the polls today for their primaries. Here are the races to watch. And, global conflicts are at their highest level since World War II, data shows.

(Image credit: Frederic J. Brown)
item.dc:creator: Brittney Melton

item.title: Conflicts around the globe hit highest levels since World War II, new report says
item.description: The new report by the Uppsala Conflict Data Program said the massacres in El Fasher pushed one-sided violence in Africa to its highest levels since the Rwandan genocide in 1994.
item.content:encoded: The new report by the Uppsala Conflict Data Program said the massacres in El Fasher pushed one-sided violence in Africa to its highest levels since the Rwandan genocide in 1994.
item.dc:creator: Emmanuel Akinwotu

item.title: Global violence reaches record levels due to war and attacks on civilians
item.description: Attacks on civilians have brought global violence to record levels. NPR's Leila Fadel talks to researcher Therése Pettersson at Uppsala University in Sweden.
item.content:encoded: Attacks on civilians have brought global violence to record levels. NPR's Leila Fadel talks to researcher Therése Pettersson at Uppsala University in Sweden.
item.dc:creator: Leila Fadel

item.title: Conflicts are on the rise globally, at the highest level since WWII, data shows
item.description: Global conflicts surged to the highest number tallied by Uppsala Conflict Data Program. Fatalities were the highest on record since 1994, with approximately 244,600 people killed in conflict in 2025.
item.content:encoded:
Global conflicts surged to the highest number tallied by Uppsala Conflict Data Program. Fatalities were the highest on record since 1994, with approximately 244,600 people killed in conflict in 2025.

(Image credit: AFP via Getty Images)
item.dc:creator: Kate Bartlett

item.title: Sweden set to ban mobile phones in schools
item.description: Long championed as a leader in adopting digital technology, Sweden is set to ban mobile phones in schools beginning in the fall for the next academic year.
item.content:encoded:
Long championed as a leader in adopting digital technology, Sweden is set to ban mobile phones in schools beginning in the fall for the next academic year.

(Image credit: James Brooks)
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press

item.title: Trump vows to respond after Iran downed a U.S. Army helicopter near Strait of Hormuz
item.description: A drone boat rescued two Army aviators who were aboard the Apache attack helicopter when it went down near the Strait of Hormuz.
item.content:encoded:
A drone boat rescued two Army aviators who were aboard the Apache attack helicopter when it went down near the Strait of Hormuz.

(Image credit: Mark Schiefelbein)
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press

item.title: Pentagon labels tech giant Alibaba and car maker BYD as aiding Chinese military
item.description: The Pentagon has added several prominent Chinese businesses to its list of Chinese military companies. The move prevents them from securing U.S. defense contracts.
item.content:encoded:
The Pentagon has added several prominent Chinese businesses to its list of Chinese military companies. The move prevents them from securing U.S. defense contracts.

(Image credit: Mahesh Kumar A.)
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press

item.title: Federal judge strikes down Trump's $100,000 fee on new H-1B visas
item.description: A federal judge on Monday struck down the Trump administration's $100,000 fee on new H-1B visas. The administration announced the fee as a way of preventing foreign workers from taking American jobs.
item.content:encoded:
A federal judge on Monday struck down the Trump administration's $100,000 fee on new H-1B visas. The administration announced the fee as a way of preventing foreign workers from taking American jobs.

(Image credit: Wilfredo Lee)
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press
